title: Office of Tax Analysis
summary: This is the Treasury Department's Office of Tax Analysis (OTA) institutional homepage, serving as a navigation index for tax policy research and data rather than a standalone research document. The page aggregates links to distributional analyses, tax rate tables, working papers, and policy studies but does not itself present new macro catalyst content or market-moving information.
key_points: This is an OTA institutional homepage/navigation page, not a standalone research publication or macro catalyst document.
The page contains links to distributional analysis of US tax system, tax expenditure distributions, and OECD effective tax rate tables.
Notable content includes recent studies on TCJA extension costs (January 2025), nonfiler withholding (January 2025), and family counts by filing status (January 2025).
No specific macro catalyst event, data release, or market-moving announcement is presented on this page itself.
The page serves as an index for accessing Treasury tax analysis publications rather than containing substantive new research.
why_it_matters: This page is not suitable for the research archive as it is a navigation/index page. It provides access points to various tax analysis documents but does not itself contain actionable macro catalyst content or market-relevant research findings.
notes: Source is a Treasury Office of Tax Analysis institutional homepage containing navigation links to various tax policy documents and data tables. The page itself is not a research publication or macro catalyst document. It contains no publication date. Per CORE RULE #6, homepage and navigation pages are non-archiveable. The OTA publishes substantive research separately (technical papers, working papers) which should be accessed directly for macro-relevant content.

reasoning: Page type is homepage/navigation - explicitly non-archiveable per CORE RULE #6
No publication date available for the page itself
No substantive macro catalyst or market structure content present
Source serves as an index to other documents rather than a standalone research output
